name.description: Project name, available in string templates as {{ name }}.

time_zone.description:
IANA timezone used to display times, available in string templates as {{ timeZone }}.
Default: "UTC"

custom_string_patterns.description: Custom string patterns to use in templates. The key is the pattern, available as '{{ <key> }}' while the resolved value is the key value.

mode.description:
Defines the execution strategy:
- "review": routes updates through a proposal (PR, MR, ...).
- "auto": bypasses the proposal and commits directly to the branch.
If choosing "auto", see `auto.trigger_strategy`.
Default: "review"

draft.description:
If enabled, the proposal will be created as draft.
Default: false

working_branch_name_template.description:
String template for branch name that Zephyr Release will use.
Allowed patterns to use are: fixed base string patterns.
Note: This value is immutable at runtime and cannot be changed via `runtime_config_override`.
Default: "zephyr-release/{{ triggerBranchName }}"

title_template.description:
String template for proposal title, using with string patterns like {{ nextVersion }}.
Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns.
Default: "chore(release): v{{ nextVersion }}"

title_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing proposal title template. Overrides `title_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

header_template.description:
String template for proposal header, using with string patterns like {{ nextVersion }}.
Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns.
Default: "# 🤖 Release Proposal"

header_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing proposal header template. Overrides `header_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

body_template.description:
String template for proposal body, using with string patterns like {{ changelogRelease }}.
Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns.
Default: "{{ changelogRelease }}"

body_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing proposal body template. Overrides `body_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

footer_template.description:
String template for proposal footer, using with string patterns.
Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns.
Default: "---\n*This proposal was generated with [Zephyr Release](https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release)*"

footer_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing proposal footer template. Overrides `footer_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

color.description:
The hexadecimal color code for the label, in standard format with the leading #.
Default: "#ededed"

The hexadecimal color code for the label, in standard format with the leading #.
Default: "#ededed"

The hexadecimal color code for the label, in standard format with the leading #.
Default: "#ededed"

The hexadecimal color code for the label, in standard format with the leading #.
Default: "#ededed"

The hexadecimal color code for the label, in standard format with the leading #.
Default: "#ededed"

The hexadecimal color code for the label, in standard format with the leading #.
Default: "#ededed"

on_merge.description:
Labels to attach and remove from proposals when merged. Can be a string, a label object, or an array containing either.
For remove, you can use "<ALL_ON_CREATE>" to remove all labels added in `on_create`.

labels.description: Labels to attach and remove from proposals on different stages.

review.description: Configuration specific to the "review" execution `mode`. Defines how release proposals (such as PRs, MRs, ...) are generated, formatted, and tracked.

type.description: Triggers a release automatically when the pushed commits contain specific allowed types.

min_commit_count.description:
The minimum number of unreleased matching commits required to trigger a release. Accepts a single number for a global count, or an object mapping specific commit types to their own minimum counts.
When using object, thresholds are evaluated using OR logic, the release triggers if ANY of the specified counts are met.
Default: 1

require_breaking.description:
If set to true, an auto-release will ONLY trigger if at least one of the matching commits contains a breaking change.
Default: false

type.description: Triggers a release automatically when a specific token is found in the commit footers.

token.description: The conventional commit footer token to look for (e.g., "Autorelease").

value.description: The specific value the footer token must have (e.g., "true"). If omitted, only the token presence is required.

type.description:
Triggers a release based on a strict boolean flag. Ideal for dynamic configuration overrides and custom script evaluations.
The strategy will be evaluated after the cmd hooks `base.pre` and `prepare.pre` run.

value.description: A hardcoded boolean flag to explicitly force or skip the release trigger.

trigger_strategy.description:
Defines the strategy that determines whether an automated release should be triggered. Used when `mode` is set to "auto".
Default: {"type":"commit-types"}

auto.description: Configuration specific to the "auto" execution `mode`. Defines the conditions and strategies for bypassing proposals and committing releases directly.

initial_version.description:
Initial SemVer version used when no existing version is found or the existing one is `0.0.0`.
You can still set this to `0.0.0` if you want it as the starting value; looping problems are handled automatically under the hood.
Default: "0.1.0"

path.description:
Path to the version file, relative to the project root.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

format.description:
Defines the file format. Allowed values: "auto", "json", "jsonc", "json5", "yaml", "toml", "txt".
Default: "auto"

extractor.description:
Defines how to extract the version from the parsed file.
Default: "auto"

selector.description: Defines how to locate the version field, depends on `extractor`.

primary.description: Marks this file as the primary source of truth for the current version.

path.description:
Path to the version file, relative to the project root.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

format.description:
Defines the file format. Allowed values: "auto", "json", "jsonc", "json5", "yaml", "toml", "txt".
Default: "auto"

extractor.description:
Defines how to extract the version from the parsed file.
Default: "auto"

selector.description: Defines how to locate the version field, depends on `extractor`.

primary.description: Marks this file as the primary source of truth for the current version.

section.description: Changelog section heading for this commit type. When not provided, `type` value is used.

section_alt.description: Changelog alternative section heading for this commit type. When not provided, `section` value is used.

hidden.description:
Exclude this commit type from changelog generation (does not affect version bump calculation).
Default: false

max_commits_to_resolve.description:
The maximum number of commits allowed to resolve, the rest will be truncated.
Default: 100

resolve_until_commit_hash.description:
Forces the tool to keep resolving commits until it reaches this specific hash, completely bypassing the standard resolve behavior.
This still respects the `max_commits_to_resolve` safety limit.
Avoid hardcoding this in your static config file, set it dynamically.

treat_major_as_minor_pre_stable.description:
Treats major changes as minor version bumps in pre-1.0 (0.x.x) releases.
Default: true

treat_minor_as_patch_pre_stable.description:
Treats minor changes as patch version bumps in pre-1.0 (0.x.x) releases.
Default: true

types.description: Commit types that count toward version bumping, must be picked from the base `commit_types` list.

count_breaking_as.description:
Count a breaking change as none, or one commit, or one bump directly regardless of current chosen `types`, as long as the commit type exists in base `commit_types` list.
Default: "commit"

commits_per_bump.description:
Number of commits required for additional version bump after the first. Use Infinity to always bump once, unless `count_breaking_as` is set to "bump".
Default: Infinity

types.description: Commit types that count toward version bumping, must be picked from the base `commit_types` list.

count_breaking_as.description:
Count a breaking change as none, or one commit, or one bump directly regardless of current chosen `types`, as long as the commit type exists in base `commit_types` list.
Default: "commit"

commits_per_bump.description:
Number of commits required for additional version bump after the first. Use Infinity to always bump once, unless `count_breaking_as` is set to "bump".
Default: Infinity

types.description: Commit types that count toward version bumping, must be picked from the base `commit_types` list.

count_breaking_as.description:
Count a breaking change as none, or one commit, or one bump directly regardless of current chosen `types`, as long as the commit type exists in base `commit_types` list.
Default: "commit"

commits_per_bump.description:
Number of commits required for additional version bump after the first. Use Infinity to always bump once, unless `count_breaking_as` is set to "bump".
Default: Infinity

enabled.description:
Enable/disable handling of SemVer extensions (pre-release identifiers / build metadata).
Default: false

treat_override_as_significant.description:
If set to `true`, the presence of an `override` is strictly treated as a structural change.
This immediately triggers resets on any dependent version components (e.g., resetting the Build number)
If `false`, overrides are treated as volatile/dynamic and ignored by reset logic.
Default: false

type.description: A stable label that should not change often. Examples: "alpha", "beta", "rc".

value.description: The static string value to use.

type.description: A label value that often changes per build or commit, usually sourced externally (e.g., git hash, branch name).

value.description: The string value to use, should be set dynamically.

fallback_value.description: The fallback string value used when `value` is empty. If this is also empty, the identifier/metadata will be omitted from the array.

initial_value.description:
Initial integer number value, will auto-increment by 1 on each bump.
Default: 0

type.description: Integer value that changes over time, representing a specific point in time since January 1, 1970 (UTC).

unit.description:
The time unit. "ms" (13 digits) or "s" (10 digits).
Default: "ms"

type.description: A date string that changes over time.

time_zone.description: The timezone to use for the date. If not specified, falls back to base `time_zone`.

extensions.description: Specifies the items to use for SemVer extensions.

enabled.description:
Enable/disable handling of SemVer extensions (pre-release identifiers / build metadata).
Default: false

treat_override_as_significant.description:
If set to `true`, the presence of an `override` is strictly treated as a structural change.
This immediately triggers resets on any dependent version components (e.g., resetting the Build number)
If `false`, overrides are treated as volatile/dynamic and ignored by reset logic.
Default: false

type.description: A stable label that should not change often. Examples: "alpha", "beta", "rc".

value.description: The static string value to use.

type.description: A label value that often changes per build or commit, usually sourced externally (e.g., git hash, branch name).

value.description: The string value to use, should be set dynamically.

fallback_value.description: The fallback string value used when `value` is empty. If this is also empty, the identifier/metadata will be omitted from the array.

initial_value.description:
Initial integer number value, will auto-increment by 1 on each bump.
Default: 0

type.description: Integer value that changes over time, representing a specific point in time since January 1, 1970 (UTC).

unit.description:
The time unit. "ms" (13 digits) or "s" (10 digits).
Default: "ms"

type.description: A date string that changes over time.

time_zone.description: The timezone to use for the date. If not specified, falls back to base `time_zone`.

extensions.description: Specifies the items to use for SemVer extensions.

bump_strategy.description: Configuration options to calculate the next version number.

write_to_file.description:
Enable/disable writing changelog to file. When disabled, changelogs are still generated for proposals, releases and string templates.
Default: true

path.description:
Path to the file where the generated changelog will be written to, relative to the project root.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional 
Default: "CHANGELOG.md"

commit_group_mode.description:
Defines how commits are sub-grouped within their respective changelog sections (Features, Fixes, etc.).
- "none": Commits are rendered as a single flat list.
- "scope-first": Commits are grouped by their scope. Scoped groups appear at the top, and unscoped commits fall to the bottom.
- "scope-last": Commits are grouped by their scope. Unscoped commits sit at the top, and scoped groups follow below.
Default: "none"

commit_sort_order.description:
Defines the sorting algorithm used to order the commits (and their groups, if a grouping mode is used).
- "alphabetical": Sorts alphabetically from A to Z.
- "newest-first": Sorts by commit timestamp, placing the newest commits at the top.
- "oldest-first": Sorts by commit timestamp, placing the oldest commits at the top.
Default: "alphabetical"

file_header_template.description:
String template for changelog file header, using with string patterns like {{ nextVersion }}. Placed above any changelog content. Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns. Default: "# Changelog\n\n
"

file_header_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing changelog file header. Overrides `file_header_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

file_release_template.description:
String template for the individual release block inserted into the changelog file.
To use your alternative configuration, set this to "{{ changelogReleaseAlt }}".
Default: "{{ changelogRelease }}"

file_release_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing changelog release template. Overrides `file_release_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

file_footer_template.description:
String template for changelog file footer, using with string patterns like {{ nextVersion }}. Placed below any changelog content.
Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns.

file_footer_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing changelog file footer. Overrides `file_footer_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

release_header_template.default:
## {{ nextVersion | wrap_compare_latest_tag: tagName }} (
    {{- YYYY }}-{{ MM }}-{{ DD }}) <!-- timezone: {{ timeZone }} -->
release_header_template.description:
String template for header of a changelog release, using with string patterns like {{ nextVersion }}.
Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns.
Default: "## {{ nextVersion | wrap_compare_latest_tag: tagName }} (\n    {{- YYYY }}-{{ MM }}-{{ DD }}) <!-- timezone: {{ timeZone }} -->"

release_header_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing changelog release header. Overrides `release_header_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

release_section_heading_template.description:
String template for heading of a changelog release section, using with string patterns like {{ section }}.
Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns.
Additionally, you can use special dynamic patterns like: {{ section }}, {{ sectionAlt }}.
Default: "### {{ section }}"

release_section_heading_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing changelog release section heading template. Overrides `release_section_heading_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

release_section_entry_template.default:
- {% if scope %}**{{ scope }}:** {% endif %}{{ desc | format_commit_references: commit }} ([{{ hash | slice: 0, 7 }}](
  {{- host }}/{{ namespace }}/{{ repository }}/{{ commitPathPart }}/{{ hash }}))
release_section_entry_template.description:
String template for each entries in the changelog release sections. Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns.
Additionally, you can use a special set of dynamic patterns which are:
{{ hash }}, {{ type }}, {{ scope }}, {{ desc }}, {{ body }}, {{ footer }}, {{ breakingDesc }}, {{ isBreaking }}, {{ authorName }}, {{ authorEmail }}, {{ authorDate }}, {{ committerName }}, {{ committerEmail }}, {{ committerDate }}.
About special patterns: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/config-options.md#changelog--release-section-entry-template-optional
Default: "- {% if scope %}**{{ scope }}:** {% endif %}{{ desc | format_commit_references: commit }} ([{{ hash | slice: 0, 7 }}](\n  {{- host }}/{{ namespace }}/{{ repository }}/{{ commitPathPart }}/{{ hash }}))"

release_section_entry_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing changelog release section entry template. Overrides `release_section_entry_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

release_breaking_section_heading.description: Heading of a changelog release BREAKING section.

release_breaking_section_entry_template.description:
Basically the same as `release_section_entry_template`, but for breaking changes specifically. If not provided, falls back to `release_section_entry_template`.
Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns.
Default: "- {% if scope %}**{{ scope }}:** {% endif %}{{ breakingDesc }}"

release_breaking_section_entry_template_.description:
Path to text file containing changelog release breaking section entry template. Overrides `release_breaking_section_entry_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

release_body_override.description: User-provided changelog release body, available in string templates as {{ changelogReleaseBody }}. If set, completely ignores the built-in generation and uses this value as the content. Should only be set dynamically, not in static config.

release_body_override_path.description:
Path to text file containing changelog release body override, will take precedence over `release_body_override`.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

release_footer_template.description:
String template for footer of a changelog release, using with string patterns.
Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns.

release_footer_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing changelog release footer. Overrides `release_footer_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

release_header_template_alt.description: Alternative value for `release_header_template`. When not provided, fall back to the original.

release_header_template_alt_path.description:
Path to text file containing alternative changelog release header. Overrides `release_header_template_alt` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

release_section_heading_template_alt.description:
String template for alternative heading of a changelog release section. Allowed string patterns and special dynamic patterns are the same as `release_section_heading_template`.
Default: "### {{ sectionAlt }}"

release_section_heading_template_alt_pat.description:
Path to text file containing alternative changelog release section heading template. Overrides `release_section_heading_template_alt` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

release_section_entry_template_alt.description: Alternative value for `release_section_entry_template`. When not provided, fall back to the original.

release_section_entry_template_alt_path.description:
Path to text file containing alternative changelog release section entry template. Overrides `release_section_entry_template_alt` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

release_breaking_section_heading_alt.description: Alternative value for `release_breaking_section_heading`. When not provided, fall back to the original.

release_breaking_section_entry_template_.description: Alternative value for `release_breaking_section_entry_template`. When not provided, fall back to the original.

Path to text file containing alternative changelog release breaking section entry template. Overrides `release_breaking_section_entry_template_alt` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

release_body_override_alt.description: Alternative value for `release_body_override`. When not provided, fall back to the original.

release_body_override_alt_path.description:
Path to text file containing alternative changelog release body override. Overrides `release_body_override_alt` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

release_footer_template_alt.description: Alternative value for `release_footer_template`. When not provided, fall back to the original.

release_footer_template_alt_path.description:
Path to text file containing alternative changelog release footer. Overrides `release_footer_template_alt` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

changelog.description: Configuration specific to changelogs. All generated changelog content are available in string templates as {{ changelogRelease }} (release header + body) or {{ changelogReleaseHeader }} and {{ changelogReleaseBody }}.

header_template.description:
String template for commit header, using with string patterns like {{ nextVersion }}. You can optionally include a CI skip token here (or body/footer) to prevent downstream pipeline runs (e.g., `[skip ci]` or `[ci skip]` for GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket).
Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns.
Default: "chore(release): v{{ nextVersion }}"

header_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing commit header template. Overrides `header_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

body_template.description:
String template for commit body, using with string patterns like {{ changelogRelease }}. You can optionally include a CI skip token here (or header/footer) to prevent downstream pipeline runs (e.g., `[skip ci]` or `[ci skip]` for GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket).
Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns.

body_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing commit body template. Overrides `body_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

footer_template.description:
String template for commit footer, using with string patterns. You can optionally include a CI skip token here (or header/body) to prevent downstream pipeline runs (e.g., `[skip ci]` or `[ci skip]` for GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket).
Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns.

footer_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing commit footer template. Overrides `footer_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

create_tag.description:
Enable/disable tag creation. If disabled, create release note will also be skipped.
Default: true

name_template.description:
String template for tag name, using with string patterns like {{ nextVersion }}. Available in string templates as {{ tagName }}.
Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns (except {{ tagName }} itself).
Default: "v{{ nextVersion }}"

type.description:
The type of Git tag to create, either lightweight, annotated or signed.
- If annotated or signed, a tag message is required.
- If signed, you must pre-configure the CI runner environment with GPG/SSH keys yourself (Zephyr Release does not manage keys for security reasons).
Default: "lightweight"

message_template.description:
String template for the Git annotated or signed tag message.
Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns.
Default: "Release {{ tagName }}"

message_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing Git annotated or signed tag message template. Overrides `message_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

name.description: The name of the tag creator.

email.description: The email of the tag creator.

date.description:
Override the Git tag timestamp.
Can be one of these options:
- 'now': The moment the operation creates the tag.
- 'commit-date': The Git committer date.
- 'author-date': The Git author date.
- Or a specific ISO 8601 date string.
If omitted, defaults to the platform native behavior (recommended).

tagger.description: Custom identity and timestamp information for the Git tag. If omitted, defaults to the platform native behavior.

prerelease.description:
If enabled, the release will be marked as prerelease.
Default: false

draft.description:
If enabled, the release will be created as draft.
Default: false

set_latest.description:
If enabled, the release will be set as the latest release.
Default: true

title_template.description:
String template for release note title, using with string patterns like {{ tagName }}.
Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns.
Default: "{{ tagName }}"

title_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing release title template. Overrides `title_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

header_template.description:
String template for release note header, using with string patterns.
Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns.

header_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing release header template. Overrides `header_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

body_template.description:
String template for release note body, using with string patterns like {{ changelogRelease }}.
Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns.
Default: "{{ changelogRelease }}"

body_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing release body template. Overrides `body_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

footer_template.description:
String template for release note footer, using with string patterns.
Allowed patterns to use are: all fixed and dynamic string patterns.

footer_template_path.description:
Path to text file containing release footer template. Overrides `footer_template` when both are provided.
To customize whether this file is fetched locally or remotely, see source mode: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/input-options.md#source-mode-optional

timeout.description:
Base default timeout (ms) for all commands in `pre` and `post`, can be overridden per command.
Use Infinity to never timeout (not recommended).
Default: 60000 (1 min)

continue_on_error.description:
Base default behavior for all commands in `pre` and `post`, can be overridden per command.
Default: false

timeout.description:
Timeout in milliseconds, use Infinity to never timeout (not recommended).
Defaults to `commandHook` base `timeout` value

continue_on_error.description:
Continue or stop the process on commands error.
Defaults to `commandHook` base `continue_on_error` value

base.description:
Pre/post commands to run around the main operation. Each command runs from the repository root.
Post commands will always run regardless of operation outcome (success, skipped or failure). It is recommended to check the outcome export variable if your script should only run under specific conditions.
Available variables that cmds can use: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/export-variables.md

Default: 60000 (1 min)

prepare.description:
Pre/post commands to run around the proposal (PR, MR, ...) operation. Each command runs from the repository root.
Available variables that cmds can use: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/export-variables.md

publish.description:
Pre/post commands to run around the release operation. Each command runs from the repository root.
Available variables that cmds can use: https://github.com/ptphongkmf/zephyr-release/blob/main/docs/export-variables.md

command_hooks.description: Command hooks to run at different phases of the operation.

runtime_config_override.description:
A dynamic configuration file to deep merge over the resolved config at runtime, typically generated by a `commandHook` script.
This file is always read from the local filesystem. If the file does not exist or empty, it is safely ignored. However, if the file exists but the merged result fails schema validation, the operation will throw an error.
Note: Some core structural fields (e.g., `review.working_branch_name_template`) are protected and cannot be overridden.

title: Zephyr Release configuration file
description: A JSON representation of a Zephyr Release configuration file.
